confirmation.detail: inbox envelope env_6424109c-2502-4a0e-89ea-7cdefd43e137 read back as promoted

payload.authority_note: Operator requested CAPA after observing the context-burning overlay inspect failure.

capa_requests.text: Add or update an operator-surface compact inspect command/helper that returns current bindings/labels in a stable documented shape for architect loops.
Make Inspect-WindowSurfaceOverlay.ps1 support a compact mode or documented properties so callers do not guess JSON shape.
Add tests ensuring overlay inspect consumers fail once with a compact schema error rather than repeating property-missing errors for every object.
Architect-loop guidance should prefer sanctioned compact inspect helpers over ad hoc Select-Object against unknown JSON.

incident.correction_taken: Architect answered from the runtime binding file instead and identified the inspect parsing as an ergonomics defect.
incident.root_cause: The caller assumed inspect JSON shape instead of using a documented compact inspect contract or schema-aware helper. The inspect command/output shape is not easy enough to consume safely from architect loops.
incident.what_happened: Architect tried to pipe Inspect-WindowSurfaceOverlay.ps1 output through ConvertFrom-Json and Select-Object -ExpandProperty labels. The inspect output did not have a labels property, causing repeated Select-Object errors and substantial transcript/context waste.

payload.summary: While checking whether the current terminal was bound to the operator surface, the architect used Inspect-WindowSurfaceOverlay.ps1 with an assumed labels property. The command emitted a large repeated PowerShell error stream instead of a compact schema-aware failure. This is a local tooling and architect-loop ergonomics CAPA.
payload.title: CAPA: schema-unaware overlay inspect parsing caused context burn
